documents.text: From LV Surgery Associate 17024428641 3/17/2026 15:14:36 PDT Page 01 of 12 From: Jiashou Xu To: Avery Home Health Email: drxu@lvsurgical.com Email: Phone: (702) 258-7788 Phone: (702) 416-0611 Fax: +1 702-442-8641 Fax: +1 702-899-4479 Patient Name: CHARLOTTE ONEIL Phone: (702) 333-1746 DOB: 10/14/1945 6120 MAVERICK ST Address Age: 80 Las Vegas, NV 89130 Sex Female Mail Address Same as address SSN Not on file Primary Insurance Secondary Insurance Company: Medicare NV Company: Tricare For Life Plan: Plan: Group #: Group #: Policy #: 6TQ5VJ4PT84 Policy #: 00136130403 Subscriber: CHARLOTTE ONEIL Subscriber: CHARLOTTE ONEIL Reason for referral: Wound care referral for packing changes Diagnoses: ICD-10 Code Description K57.20 Diverticulitis of large intestine with perforation and abscess without bleeding Mrs Provider: Jiashou Xu Date: 03/17/26 03:14 PM From LV Surgery Associate 17024428641 3/17/2026 15:14:36 PDT Page 02 of 12 Patient: CHARLOTTE ONEIL DOB: 10/14/1945 Sex: F Provider: Victor Yoon Visit: 03/17/2026 11:45AM Chart: ONCH07CF30B4 Chief Complaint: Hospital Post-Op History of Present Illness: Ostomy productive, tolerating diet. Midportion of wound has opened up. Physical Exam: NAD Soft abdomen, ostomy productive and pink, small ~3cm open of the midline wound midportion and upon probing extends some inferiorly and to the level of abdominal wall which feels intact. No significant purulence. Assessment: s/p robotic converted to open LAR, end colostomy Type Code Description ICD-10-CM Condition K57.20 Diverticulitis of large Intestine with perforation and abscess without bleeding ICD-10-CM Condition R10.9 Unspecified abdominal pain ICD-10-CM Condition R11.10 Vomiting, unspecified Plan: Penrose removed Some staples removed, but left the majority Wound care referral for packing changes Follow up 2 weeks Type Code Modifiers Quantity Description CPT 99024 1.00 UN POSTOP FOLLOW-UP VISIT Medications & Allergies: Allergy Reaction codeine aspirin E-signed by Victor Yoon on 03/17/2026 12:16PM PDT Powered by [Page 1 of 1] This page was generated at 03/17/2026 12:16PM PDT DrChrono From LV Surgery Associate 17024428641 3/17/2026 15:14:36 PDT Page 03 of 12 Operative Reports ONEIL, CHARLOTTE ANN - CHH7329836 * Final Report * colon was sharply excised, and passed off the table with the specimen. The colostomy was then matured in Brooke fashion using sutures of 3-0 Vicryl. Ostomy appliance was placed. This concluded the procedure. All counts were correct. I was present for the entire procedure. Signature Line Electronically Signed By: Yoon, Victor DO On: 02.20.2026 19:20 PST Completed Action List: * Perform by Yoon DO, Victor on February 20, 2026 13:34 PST * Modify by Yoon DO, Victor on February 20, 2026 19:20 PST * Sign by Yoon DO, Victor on February 20, 2026 19:20 PST Requested by Yoon DO, Victor on February 20, 2026 13:34 PST * VERIFY by Yoon DO, Victor on February 20, 2026 19:20 PST Printed by: Guhin, Karen Printed on: 3/16/2026 11:09 PDT Page 3 of 3 From LV Surgery Associate 17024428641 3/17/2026 15:14:36 PDT Page 04 of 12 Operative Reports ONEIL, CHARLOTTE ANN - CHH7329836 * Final Report * There was a large subcostal incision in the left upper quadrant, and decision was made to enter the abdomen in the right upper quadrant. Incision was made in the right upper quadrant to accommodate a robotic trocar. Veress needle was inserted and the abdomen was then insufflated to a pressure of 15 mmHg. The patient tolerated insufflation well. Veress needle was removed and a robotic trocar was inserted. Robotic camera was inserted and the abdomen was inspected. There were copious abdominal adhesions, there were colonic and omental adhesions to the midline, as well as the incisional hernia noted, I tried to maneuver around the incisions, I noticed that the sigmoid was very densely adherent to its surroundings, decision was made to perform an open procedure. Robotic camera was removed. Midline incision was made from the epigastric-area to the suprapubic area. Subcutaneous tissues were divided using electrocautery, and the midportion of the abdomen, incisional hernia was encountered, and hernia sac was dissected free from its subcutaneous attachments down to the level of fascia. Hernia sac was sharply incised, the contents were reduced, I was then able to incise the fascia and this was achieved by dissecting thickened omental adhesions as well as colonic adhesions from the midline, and then incising fascia in a serial manner. Once the midline was cleared and the fascia had been opened, I was then able to dissect omental as well as small bowel adhesions to the anterior abdominal wall and bring these down. The omentum was then reflected cephalad, Bookwalter retractor was placed. Small bowel was densely adherent to the pelvis, and small bowel adhesions were sharply lysed. There were 2 areas of inadvertent serosal injury, secondary to very dense adhesions, and these were repaired using sutures of 3-0 silk in Lembert fashion. Small bowel was then retracted cephalad. The left colon was then mobilized along the line of Toldt, colonic mesentery and retroperitoneum were separated along the avascular plane, the sigmoid was noted to be very inflamed, thickened. I mobilized the sigmoid away from the pelvis by using sharp dissection, as it was very densely adherent to this area. The proximal rectum and distal sigmoid were plastered to the pelvis, anterior pelvis, and I needed to perform finger fracture in order to separate the colon from these areas. Once I was able to mobilize the sigmoid, the proximal rectum was also extremely adherent to its surroundings, and I had to further finger fracture this area away from its pelvic attachments. Once this was completed, I was able to reach the soft portion of the rectum, which was in the midportion. A window was created in the mesentery of the descending colon, and the colon divided using a contour stapler and a green load. The mesentery was then taken down to the proximal rectum using Ligasure, some of the mesorectum was divided in order for us to get down to the mid rectum for stapler. Contour stapler was then used to divide the mid rectum, using a green load. The low anterior resection specimen was then passed off the table. Bleeding from the sigmoid mesentery was oversewn using 2-0 silk in figure-of-eight fashion. Hemostasis was satisfactory. The rectal stump was tagged with a 2-0 Prolene. The left colon was then mobilized further along the white line of Toldt, separating mesentery from retroperitoneum along the avascular plane, up to the level of the splenic flexure. There is significant scarring in the area secondary to the patient's prior left nephrectomy. The splenic flexure was mobilized from attachments using sharp and blunt dissection. The gastrocolic ligament was opened, and this allowed me to fully mobilize the splenic flexure. Hemostasis was satisfactory. Circular left lower quadrant incision was then made for ostomy site. A column was seen and subcutaneous tissue was sharply excised and discarded. The fascia was incised in a cruciate manner, the rectus muscle was split and the remainder of the abdominal wall was incised using electrocautery. The colon was then brought through the ostomy site, care was taken to avoid twisting. No tension was noted on the colostomy. Bilateral myocutaneous flaps were then raised so that there could be at tension-free closure. This was done by incising the subcutaneous tissue away from the anterior fascia in all directions. The abdomen was then copiously irrigated and free fluid was suctioned. Hemostasis was satisfactory. 19 French channel drain was placed in the pelvis and left paracolic gutter and exited out a right lower quadrant stab incision. The drain was secured using nylon. There was still some hernia sac present and this was sharply excised free down to the level of the fascia and discarded. The abdomen was then closed using 2 sutures of 1 looped PDS in running fashion. About every 3 to 4 cm a widely spaced suture of 1 Vicryl in interrupted fashion was placed in order to serve as internal retention sutures, thereby completing our complex closure, and also completing primary repair of the patient's incisional hernia. 4000 mg of Restrata Minimatrix was placed into the subcutaneous space in order to mitigate infection and promote wound healing. Penrose drain was placed at the base of the wound and exited out the inferior portion of the incision. The skin was closed using skin staples. The Penrose drain was secured to the skin using a stitch of Vicryl. Skin staples were used to close the right upper quadrant incision. Dry clean dressings were placed. Next we turned our attention to maturing the ostomy. The colon was very thickened at the ostomy, a couple centimeters of Printed by: Guhin, Karen Printed on: 3/16/2026 11:09 PDT Page 2 of 3 From LV Surgery Associate 17024428641 3/17/2026 15:14:36 PDT Page 05 of 12 Operative Reports ONEIL, CHARLOTTE ANN - CHH7329836 * Final Report * Result type: Operative Reports Result date: February 20, 2026 13:28 PST Result status: Auth (Verified) Result title: Post Operative Note Performed by: Yoon DO, Victor on February 20, 2026 13:34 PST Verified by: Yoon DO, Victor on February 20, 2026 19:20 PST Encounter info: CHH8101158650, CHH Center, Inpatient, 2/16/2026 - 3/6/2026 * Final Report * Indication for Surgery 80F with prior history of left nephrectomy and cholecystectomy, presented after recent admission with recurrent diverticulitis. She had intractable pain and failure of medical management and she was taken for OR. Preoperative Diagnosis Diverticulitis Postoperative Diagnosis Same Operation ROBOTIC ASSITED DIAGNOSTIC LAPAROSCOPY, CONVERTED TO OPEN LOW ANTERIOR RESECTION, EXTENSIVE LYSIS OF ADHESIONS 40MINUTES, REPAIR OF SMALL BOWEL SEROSAL INJURY X 2, MOBILIZATION OF SPLENIC FLEXURE, END COLOSTOMY, BILATERAL MYOCUTANEOUS FLAPS, PRIMARY REPAIR OF INCISIONAL HERNIA 5CM DEFECT, COMPLEX CLOSURE WITH INTERNAL RETENTION SUTURES, RESTRATA 4000MG TO SUBCUTANEOUS SPACE Surgeon(s) Yoon DO, Victor (Surgeon) Assistant Lythgoe, Stacey (NP First Assist) Presha, Henry (Certified Tech First Assist) Anesthesia Type and Anesthesiologist General Boyle CRNA, Joshua W (Provider) Blood Loss Estimate Blood Loss: 100 mL (02/20/26 12:55:00) Urine Output 500.0 mL Findings Copious intra-abdominal adhesions, extremely dense adhesions of sigmoid and proximal rectum to the pelvis, inflamed and thickened sigmoid and proximal rectum Specimen(s) Low anterior resection Complications None Technique The patient was brought to the operating room and laid supine on operating table. General anesthesia was administered. Foley catheter was placed and arms were tucked. Zosyn IV was administered for surgical site prophylaxis. SCDs were utilized for DVT prophylaxis. The patient was prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion. A timeout was performed verifying correct patient, procedure, site, position, equipment prior to beginning. Washington Avenue. Address: 6120 MAVERICK ST Reported: 02/25/2026 Time: 04:52pm 6900 N DURANGO DR City: LAS VEGAS DOB: 10/14/1945 Age: 80 State: NV Zip Code: 89130-1381 Gender: F LAS VEGAS, NV 89149 PT Phone: (702)333-1746 Client Acc#: Phone: (702) 629-1300 Fax: MRN: CHH7329836 Visit #: NVBDR0-19973455117 Other Phys(s): VICTOR YOON, DO SAROJ LOHANI, M.D. TALHA JILANI, M.D. SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT SPECIMEN(s): COLON, SEGMENTAL RESECTION, LOWER ANTERIOR SPECIMEN CLINICAL INFORMATION: CLINICAL HISTORY: Diverticulitis ICD10 CODE(S): Not provided PROCEDURE/FINDINGS: Not provided FINAL' DIAGNOSIS: COLON, LOWER ANTERIOR RESECTION: DIVERTICULITIS WITH MUCOSAL EROSION, SUBMUCOSAL ABSCESS, AND PERFORATION. THE MARGINS ARE VIABLE. NO DYSPLASIA OR MALIGNANCY IDENTIFIED. Michelle McNamara M.D. Pathologist (Case signed 02/25/2026) MICROSCOPIC: Microscopic examination performed and the results incorporated into the final diagnosis. All controls stained appropriately. GROSS DESCRIPTION: The specimen is received in formalin, labeled with the proper patient identification and "low anterior colon section" consists of 19.3 X 3.4 cm in diameter segment of colon with surrounding pericolonic adipose tissue. The serosal surfaces are gray-tan to brown, diffusely congested with adhesions and there are three, adjacent 0.7 to 2.7 cm transmural defects, which is 3.8 cm to the nearest resection margin. The specimen is sectioned revealing a gray-tan, redundantly folded mucosa with diffuse diverticula and a thickened colon wall with two possible areas of perforation, which are inked black. Additionally found within the container is a 2.4 cm in length X 2.2 cm in diameter additional segment of colon which has a gray-pink and glistening mucosa. The specimen is representatively sampled as follows: A1-resection margins A2-transmural defects A3-A4-diverticula A5-A6-possible perforation A7-additional segment of colon (SW) Technical Component was performed at LMC PATHOLOGY SERVICES 7455W.
